articles.headline: India and New Zealand Confirm FTA Plans for 2026, Boosting Bilateral Trade
articles.explainer:
• India and New Zealand have officially announced their intention to sign a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in 2026, marking a significant step in enhancing economic ties.
• The agreement aims to reduce tariffs, streamline customs procedures, and open new market access opportunities for goods and services.
• This development follows several rounds of negotiations focusing on sectors like agriculture, dairy, services, and digital trade.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade, currently modest, and diversify India's trade basket in the Oceania region.
• It aligns with India's 'Act East' policy and broader strategy of forging closer economic partnerships with strategically important countries.

articles.trivia: New Zealand was the first country to grant women the right to vote in 1893.

articles.headline: Violent Clashes in Karbi Anglong, Assam: Two Dead, Several Injured
articles.explainer:
• Two individuals were killed and several others sustained injuries in violent clashes reported from Karbi Anglong district in Assam.
• The clashes are believed to have erupted due to long-standing land disputes and ethnic tensions between different communities in the region.
• Local administration has imposed prohibitory orders under Section 144 CrPC and deployed additional security forces to restore peace.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenges of ethnic strife and land-related conflicts in certain parts of India's North-Eastern states.
• State government has initiated an inquiry into the matter and appealed for calm, promising swift action against perpetrators.

articles.trivia: Karbi Anglong is one of the largest districts in Assam and is known for its diverse tribal population.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Grants Bail to Kuldeep Singh Sengar in 2017 Unnao Rape Case
articles.explainer:
• The Delhi High Court has granted bail to former BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar, the main accused in the 2017 Unnao rape case.
• Sengar was convicted in 2019 for the rape of a minor girl and sentenced to life imprisonment.
• The bail was granted on grounds related to his appeal pending before the Supreme Court and the period of incarceration already served.
• The decision has sparked widespread public concern and debate regarding the pace of justice delivery and the safety of women and children in India.
• This case has been a high-profile example highlighting challenges in prosecuting powerful individuals and ensuring victim protection.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes National Urban Development Authority Bill, 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Parliament has successfully passed the National Urban Development Authority (NUDA) Bill, 2025, aiming to streamline urban planning and development.
• The bill proposes the establishment of a unified national authority to oversee urban development policies, infrastructure projects, and smart city initiatives across states.
• Key objectives include fostering sustainable urban growth, ensuring equitable access to amenities, and enhancing the resilience of Indian cities to climate change.
• It seeks to address the fragmentation of urban governance by promoting integrated planning and coordination among various agencies.
• The legislation is expected to play a crucial role in managing India's rapid urbanization and achieving the vision of 'New Urban India'.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo in Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Review Amidst Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its bi-monthly Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, opting to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to managing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, prioritizing financial stability over aggressive rate adjustments.
• The policy stance reflects concerns about external headwinds potentially impacting India's domestic economic recovery trajectory.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India Hosts 'Global Counter-Terrorism Summit', Calls for Unified International Action
articles.explainer:
• India successfully hosted the 'Global Counter-Terrorism Summit' in New Delhi, bringing together leaders and security experts from over 50 nations.
• Prime Minister emphasized the need for a unified global approach to combat terrorism, transcending political differences and national boundaries.
• Discussions focused on emerging threats, including cyber-terrorism, radicalization through social media, and the financing of terror organizations.
• A 'Delhi Declaration' was adopted, outlining commitments to intelligence sharing, capacity building, and strengthening international legal frameworks against terrorism.
• The summit underscored India's growing leadership role in global security and its consistent advocacy for a zero-tolerance approach to terrorism.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down prototype demonstrating autonomous landing capabilities under simulated orbital re-entry conditions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle, aiming to reduce space launch costs dramatically.
• Reusable launch vehicles are considered essential for future space exploration, satellite deployment, and potential space tourism.
• ISRO's indigenous development of this technology reinforces India's position as a leading space power with advanced capabilities.

articles.headline: India Launches 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Mission'
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched the 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Mission' (NMPRM).
• The mission aims to conserve existing mangrove forests and undertake extensive restoration efforts in degraded areas along India's coastline.
• It integrates scientific research, community participation, and policy interventions to ensure the long-term health of these vital ecosystems.
• Mangroves play a crucial role in coastal protection, biodiversity conservation, and carbon sequestration, making them critical for climate resilience.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments under international conventions like the Ramsar Convention and its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Releases Guidelines for New National Curriculum Framework Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education has released detailed implementation guidelines for the new National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-SE), 2025.
• The framework aims to bring transformative changes in pedagogical practices, assessment methods, and overall learning experiences for students from foundational to secondary stages.
• Key features include a multidisciplinary approach, focus on critical thinking and experiential learning, and integration of vocational education from an early age.
• The guidelines emphasize teacher training, development of new textbooks, and collaboration between central and state education boards for phased implementation.
• This initiative is a cornerstone of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, seeking to align India's education system with global standards and future workforce demands.

articles.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Target, Minister Confirms
articles.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Power and New & Renewable Energy announced that India is well on its way to exceed its ambitious 2030 renewable energy targets.
• Significant progress has been made in solar and wind energy capacity additions, driven by supportive policies, technological advancements, and private sector investments.
• The country has already crossed a major milestone in non-fossil fuel installed electricity capacity, demonstrating its commitment to climate action.
• This achievement is crucial for India's energy security, reducing its reliance on fossil fuel imports, and meeting its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• Further investments in grid modernization, energy storage, and green hydrogen are being prioritized to sustain this momentum.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Legality of Electoral Bond Scheme
articles.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court has decided to review the legality and constitutional validity of the Electoral Bond Scheme.
• The scheme, introduced in 2018, allows for anonymous donations to political parties, raising concerns about transparency in political funding.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme infringes upon citizens' 'right to know' and promotes opacity in electoral finance, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government has defended the scheme, citing its aim to curb black money in elections and ensure donor privacy.
• The outcome of this review is expected to have significant implications for electoral reforms and political transparency in India.

articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India's First Private Sector Jet Engine Manufacturing Unit Inaugurated
articles.explainer:
• India's first fully private sector-led jet engine manufacturing unit has been inaugurated in Bengaluru, marking a major milestone in indigenous defense production.
• The facility, a collaboration between a leading Indian conglomerate and an international aerospace giant, will produce advanced jet engines for both military and civilian aircraft.
• This initiative is a direct outcome of the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' campaigns, aiming to reduce dependence on foreign imports for critical defense components.
• It is expected to create thousands of high-skilled jobs, foster technological expertise, and boost India's capabilities in the global aerospace supply chain.
• The unit will initially focus on components and later move to full engine assembly and testing, enhancing India's strategic autonomy.

articles.headline: India-Africa Youth Exchange Program Launched to Foster Cultural Ties
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs, in collaboration with African Union nations, has launched a comprehensive 'India-Africa Youth Exchange Program'.
• The program aims to promote cultural understanding, foster academic collaboration, and build stronger people-to-people connections between India and African countries.
• It will involve student exchange scholarships, youth leadership workshops, cultural festivals, and joint research projects.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to strengthening its strategic partnership with Africa, focusing on human resource development and youth empowerment.
• The program is expected to create a new generation of leaders and professionals with a deeper appreciation for the diverse cultures and shared aspirations of both regions.

articles.headline: Delhi Implements Emergency Measures as Air Quality Dips to 'Severe' Category
articles.explainer:
• Delhi's air quality has once again plummeted to the 'Severe' category, prompting authorities to implement stringent emergency measures under the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP).
• These measures include a ban on construction activities, restrictions on polluting vehicles, closure of schools, and advisories for residents to stay indoors.
• The dip in air quality is attributed to a combination of meteorological factors, stubble burning in neighboring states, and local emission sources.
• Public health warnings have been issued, highlighting the severe health risks associated with prolonged exposure to hazardous air pollution levels.
• The recurring crisis underscores the persistent challenges in implementing long-term solutions and inter-state cooperation for air pollution control in the National Capital Region (NCR).

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Recognition at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received widespread acclaim at a recent United Nations forum.
• Delegations from several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting similar frameworks to accelerate their digital transformation and financial inclusion efforts.
• The DPI model has been lauded for its ability to provide inclusive, interoperable, and cost-effective digital services to a vast population.
• India presented its success stories in digital identity, payments, and data exchange, emphasizing the potential for DPI to achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
• This global recognition positions India as a leader in digital governance and a key contributor to building a more digitally inclusive world.

articles.headline: New Archaeological Findings Shed Light on Late Harappan Period's Decline
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Haryana have unearthed new evidence providing fresh insights into the decline of the Late Harappan period.
• The findings, including changes in settlement patterns, agricultural practices, and material culture, suggest a complex interplay of environmental shifts and societal adaptations.
• Researchers propose that prolonged droughts and changes in river courses played a significant role, forcing communities to migrate and adopt more localized subsistence strategies.
• This new data challenges simplistic theories of a sudden collapse and points towards a more gradual and regionalized transition during the decline phase of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The study emphasizes the importance of interdisciplinary research, combining archaeology, paleoclimatology, and geology, to understand ancient civilizations.

articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists meticulously excavating an ancient site.
articles.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization is one of the world's three early civilizations of the Ancient Near East and South Asia.

articles.headline: PM Launches 'PM-Aashray Yojana' for Integrated Development of Marginalized Communities
articles.explainer:
• Prime Minister launched the 'PM-Aashray Yojana', a new central sector scheme aimed at the integrated development and empowerment of particularly marginalized communities.
• The scheme focuses on providing access to basic amenities, livelihood opportunities, education, and healthcare tailored to the specific needs of these vulnerable groups.
• It adopts a multi-pronged approach, including skill development, housing assistance, and social security benefits, to uplift communities facing systemic discrimination.
• The 'PM-Aashray Yojana' emphasizes community participation and convergence of existing government programs to ensure effective delivery and sustainable impact.
• This initiative reflects the government's commitment to 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas' and addressing disparities to achieve inclusive growth.

articles.headline: India's First Graphene Innovation Centre Inaugurated in Kerala
articles.explainer:
• India's first Graphene Innovation Centre (GIC) has been inaugurated in Kerala, marking a significant step towards advanced material research and development.
• The centre will focus on developing applications of graphene in areas such as electronics, energy storage, sensors, and biomedical devices.
• Graphene, known for its exceptional strength, conductivity, and lightweight properties, is considered a 'wonder material' with vast potential across industries.
• This initiative aims to foster a vibrant ecosystem for graphene-based startups, facilitate technology transfer, and position India as a key player in the global graphene market.
• The GIC is a collaborative effort involving central and state governments, academic institutions, and industry partners.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiative' to More Districts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced the expansion of the 'Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiative' to an additional 100 districts across India.
• The initiative focuses on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices among farmers.
• It aims to enhance the adaptive capacity of Indian agriculture to climate change impacts, such as extreme weather events and shifting rainfall patterns.
• Key components include providing climate information services, facilitating access to improved seeds, and promoting integrated farming systems.
• This expansion is crucial for ensuring food security, improving farmers' livelihoods, and achieving sustainable agricultural development in the face of a changing climate.

articles.headline: India's First Underwater Metro Expansion Project Nears Completion
articles.explainer:
• The expansion project for India's first underwater metro line, located in Kolkata, is nearing its final stages of completion.
• This ambitious project involves extending the existing metro network beneath the Hooghly River, connecting key areas of the city and its suburbs.
• The underwater tunnel section utilizes advanced engineering techniques to ensure safety, durability, and minimal environmental impact.
• Once fully operational, it will significantly reduce travel time, alleviate traffic congestion, and enhance urban mobility for millions of commuters.
• The successful completion of this project sets a precedent for future complex infrastructure developments in India's metropolitan cities.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover Three New Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists has announced the discovery of three new species of amphibians and reptiles in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats region.
• The new findings underscore the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats, which is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
• These discoveries highlight the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the unique and endemic flora and fauna of the region.
• Researchers emphasize the critical role of systematic biodiversity surveys and genetic studies in identifying unknown species and understanding ecosystem health.
• The discoveries contribute valuable data to global biodiversity databases and inform conservation strategies against habitat loss and climate change.

articles.headline: India's First AI-Powered Legal Research Platform Launched for Judiciary and Lawyers
articles.explainer:
• India's first Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered legal research platform has been launched, aiming to revolutionize legal research for judges and lawyers.
• The platform leverages machine learning algorithms to analyze vast databases of legal judgments, statutes, and articles, providing quick and accurate insights.
• Features include predictive analysis for case outcomes, automated summarization of complex legal documents, and intelligent search capabilities.
• This innovation is expected to significantly enhance efficiency in the legal profession, reduce research time, and improve access to justice through faster proceedings.
• The development aligns with the government's push for digital transformation in the judiciary and promoting AI adoption across sectors.

articles.headline: Indian Railways' First High-Speed Rail Corridor Achieves 70% Completion
articles.explainer:
• The Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail (MAHSR) corridor, India's first bullet train project, has achieved 70% physical completion, announced the National High-Speed Rail Corporation Limited (NHSRCL).
• The project involves advanced Japanese Shinkansen technology, aiming for speeds up to 320 kmph, drastically reducing travel time between the two major cities.
• Significant progress has been made on viaducts, bridges, tunnels, and station construction, with meticulous attention to safety and quality standards.
• This ambitious infrastructure project is expected to boost economic development along the corridor, create employment opportunities, and modernize India's railway network.
• The successful implementation of this project is crucial for paving the way for future high-speed rail networks across the country.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recently announced India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement (FTA), consider the following statements:
1. The FTA aims to reduce tariffs and streamline customs procedures between the two countries.
2. Key sectors of focus for this agreement include agriculture, dairy, and digital trade.
3. This agreement is a part of India's 'Look West' policy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the FTA aims to reduce tariffs and streamline customs procedures. Statement 2 is correct as agriculture, dairy, services, and digital trade are key sectors. Statement 3 is incorrect. The FTA aligns with India's 'Act East' policy and broader strategy of forging closer economic partnerships, not 'Look West'.

prelimsMocks.question:
The National Urban Development Authority (NUDA) Bill, 2025, recently passed by Parliament, aims to achieve which of the following?
1. Establish a unified national authority for urban planning.
2. Promote sustainable urban growth and smart city initiatives.
3. Address the fragmentation of urban governance.
4. Focus exclusively on infrastructure development in Tier-1 cities.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct objectives of the NUDA Bill, 2025. The bill aims to establish a unified authority, promote sustainable urban growth, smart cities, and address fragmentation in urban governance. Statement 4 is incorrect as the bill aims for comprehensive urban development across cities, not exclusively Tier-1 cities or only infrastructure.

prelimsMocks.question:

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of ISRO's recently tested next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology?

GS1.headline: New Archaeological Findings Shed Light on Late Harappan Period's Decline
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations in Haryana have unearthed new evidence providing fresh insights into the decline of the Late Harappan period.
• The findings, including changes in settlement patterns, agricultural practices, and material culture, suggest a complex interplay of environmental shifts and societal adaptations.
• Researchers propose that prolonged droughts and changes in river courses played a significant role, forcing communities to migrate and adopt more localized subsistence strategies.
• This new data challenges simplistic theories of a sudden collapse and points towards a more gradual and regionalized transition during the decline phase of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• The study emphasizes the importance of interdisciplinary research, combining archaeology, paleoclimatology, and geology, to understand ancient civilizations.

• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade, currently modest, and diversify India's trade basket in the Oceania region.
• It aligns with India's 'Act East' policy and broader strategy of forging closer economic partnerships with strategically important countries.

• Sengar was convicted in 2019 for the rape of a minor girl and sentenced to life imprisonment.
• The bail was granted on grounds related to his appeal pending before the Supreme Court and the period of incarceration already served.
• The decision has sparked widespread public concern and debate regarding the pace of justice delivery and the safety of women and children in India.
• This case has been a high-profile example highlighting challenges in prosecuting powerful individuals and ensuring victim protection.

• The bill proposes the establishment of a unified national authority to oversee urban development policies, infrastructure projects, and smart city initiatives across states.
• Key objectives include fostering sustainable urban growth, ensuring equitable access to amenities, and enhancing the resilience of Indian cities to climate change.
• It seeks to address the fragmentation of urban governance by promoting integrated planning and coordination among various agencies.
• The legislation is expected to play a crucial role in managing India's rapid urbanization and achieving the vision of 'New Urban India'.

• Prime Minister emphasized the need for a unified global approach to combat terrorism, transcending political differences and national boundaries.
• Discussions focused on emerging threats, including cyber-terrorism, radicalization through social media, and the financing of terror organizations.
• A 'Delhi Declaration' was adopted, outlining commitments to intelligence sharing, capacity building, and strengthening international legal frameworks against terrorism.
• The summit underscored India's growing leadership role in global security and its consistent advocacy for a zero-tolerance approach to terrorism.

• Key features include a multidisciplinary approach, focus on critical thinking and experiential learning, and integration of vocational education from an early age.
• The guidelines emphasize teacher training, development of new textbooks, and collaboration between central and state education boards for phased implementation.
• This initiative is a cornerstone of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, seeking to align India's education system with global standards and future workforce demands.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Legality of Electoral Bond Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court has decided to review the legality and constitutional validity of the Electoral Bond Scheme.
• The scheme, introduced in 2018, allows for anonymous donations to political parties, raising concerns about transparency in political funding.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme infringes upon citizens' 'right to know' and promotes opacity in electoral finance, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The government has defended the scheme, citing its aim to curb black money in elections and ensure donor privacy.
• The outcome of this review is expected to have significant implications for electoral reforms and political transparency in India.

• The 'PM-Aashray Yojana' emphasizes community participation and convergence of existing government programs to ensure effective delivery and sustainable impact.
• This initiative reflects the government's commitment to 'Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Vishwas' and addressing disparities to achieve inclusive growth.

GS3.headline: Violent Clashes in Karbi Anglong, Assam: Two Dead, Several Injured
GS3.explainer:
• Two individuals were killed and several others sustained injuries in violent clashes reported from Karbi Anglong district in Assam.
• The clashes are believed to have erupted due to long-standing land disputes and ethnic tensions between different communities in the region.
• Local administration has imposed prohibitory orders under Section 144 CrPC and deployed additional security forces to restore peace.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenges of ethnic strife and land-related conflicts in certain parts of India's North-Eastern states.
• State government has initiated an inquiry into the matter and appealed for calm, promising swift action against perpetrators.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo in Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Review Amidst Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its bi-monthly Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, opting to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating commodity prices.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to managing inflation within the target band while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a cautious 'wait and watch' approach, prioritizing financial stability over aggressive rate adjustments.
• The policy stance reflects concerns about external headwinds potentially impacting India's domestic economic recovery trajectory.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test flight for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down prototype demonstrating autonomous landing capabilities under simulated orbital re-entry conditions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) launch vehicle, aiming to reduce space launch costs dramatically.
• Reusable launch vehicles are considered essential for future space exploration, satellite deployment, and potential space tourism.
• ISRO's indigenous development of this technology reinforces India's position as a leading space power with advanced capabilities.

GS3.headline: India Launches 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Mission'
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched the 'National Mangrove Protection and Restoration Mission' (NMPRM).
• The mission aims to conserve existing mangrove forests and undertake extensive restoration efforts in degraded areas along India's coastline.
• It integrates scientific research, community participation, and policy interventions to ensure the long-term health of these vital ecosystems.
• Mangroves play a crucial role in coastal protection, biodiversity conservation, and carbon sequestration, making them critical for climate resilience.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments under international conventions like the Ramsar Convention and its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).

GS3.headline: India on Track to Exceed 2030 Renewable Energy Target, Minister Confirms
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Power and New & Renewable Energy announced that India is well on its way to exceed its ambitious 2030 renewable energy targets.
• Significant progress has been made in solar and wind energy capacity additions, driven by supportive policies, technological advancements, and private sector investments.
• The country has already crossed a major milestone in non-fossil fuel installed electricity capacity, demonstrating its commitment to climate action.
• This achievement is crucial for India's energy security, reducing its reliance on fossil fuel imports, and meeting its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• Further investments in grid modernization, energy storage, and green hydrogen are being prioritized to sustain this momentum.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector Jet Engine Manufacturing Unit Inaugurated
GS3.explainer:
• India's first fully private sector-led jet engine manufacturing unit has been inaugurated in Bengaluru, marking a major milestone in indigenous defense production.
• The facility, a collaboration between a leading Indian conglomerate and an international aerospace giant, will produce advanced jet engines for both military and civilian aircraft.
• This initiative is a direct outcome of the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' campaigns, aiming to reduce dependence on foreign imports for critical defense components.
• It is expected to create thousands of high-skilled jobs, foster technological expertise, and boost India's capabilities in the global aerospace supply chain.
• The unit will initially focus on components and later move to full engine assembly and testing, enhancing India's strategic autonomy.

GS3.headline: Delhi Implements Emergency Measures as Air Quality Dips to 'Severe' Category
GS3.explainer:
• Delhi's air quality has once again plummeted to the 'Severe' category, prompting authorities to implement stringent emergency measures under the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP).
• These measures include a ban on construction activities, restrictions on polluting vehicles, closure of schools, and advisories for residents to stay indoors.
• The dip in air quality is attributed to a combination of meteorological factors, stubble burning in neighboring states, and local emission sources.
• Public health warnings have been issued, highlighting the severe health risks associated with prolonged exposure to hazardous air pollution levels.
• The recurring crisis underscores the persistent challenges in implementing long-term solutions and inter-state cooperation for air pollution control in the National Capital Region (NCR).

GS3.headline: India's First Graphene Innovation Centre Inaugurated in Kerala
GS3.explainer:
• India's first Graphene Innovation Centre (GIC) has been inaugurated in Kerala, marking a significant step towards advanced material research and development.
• The centre will focus on developing applications of graphene in areas such as electronics, energy storage, sensors, and biomedical devices.
• Graphene, known for its exceptional strength, conductivity, and lightweight properties, is considered a 'wonder material' with vast potential across industries.
• This initiative aims to foster a vibrant ecosystem for graphene-based startups, facilitate technology transfer, and position India as a key player in the global graphene market.
• The GIC is a collaborative effort involving central and state governments, academic institutions, and industry partners.

GS3.headline: Government Expands 'Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiative' to More Districts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare announced the expansion of the 'Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiative' to an additional 100 districts across India.
• The initiative focuses on promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and climate-smart farming practices among farmers.
• It aims to enhance the adaptive capacity of Indian agriculture to climate change impacts, such as extreme weather events and shifting rainfall patterns.
• Key components include providing climate information services, facilitating access to improved seeds, and promoting integrated farming systems.
• This expansion is crucial for ensuring food security, improving farmers' livelihoods, and achieving sustainable agricultural development in the face of a changing climate.

GS3.headline: India's First Underwater Metro Expansion Project Nears Completion
GS3.explainer:
• The expansion project for India's first underwater metro line, located in Kolkata, is nearing its final stages of completion.
• This ambitious project involves extending the existing metro network beneath the Hooghly River, connecting key areas of the city and its suburbs.
• The underwater tunnel section utilizes advanced engineering techniques to ensure safety, durability, and minimal environmental impact.
• Once fully operational, it will significantly reduce travel time, alleviate traffic congestion, and enhance urban mobility for millions of commuters.
• The successful completion of this project sets a precedent for future complex infrastructure developments in India's metropolitan cities.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover Three New Species in Western Ghats, Highlighting Biodiversity Hotspot
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists has announced the discovery of three new species of amphibians and reptiles in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats region.
• The new findings underscore the immense ecological significance of the Western Ghats, which is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
• These discoveries highlight the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the unique and endemic flora and fauna of the region.
• Researchers emphasize the critical role of systematic biodiversity surveys and genetic studies in identifying unknown species and understanding ecosystem health.
• The discoveries contribute valuable data to global biodiversity databases and inform conservation strategies against habitat loss and climate change.

GS3.headline: India's First AI-Powered Legal Research Platform Launched for Judiciary and Lawyers
GS3.explainer:
• India's first Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered legal research platform has been launched, aiming to revolutionize legal research for judges and lawyers.
• The platform leverages machine learning algorithms to analyze vast databases of legal judgments, statutes, and articles, providing quick and accurate insights.
• Features include predictive analysis for case outcomes, automated summarization of complex legal documents, and intelligent search capabilities.
• This innovation is expected to significantly enhance efficiency in the legal profession, reduce research time, and improve access to justice through faster proceedings.
• The development aligns with the government's push for digital transformation in the judiciary and promoting AI adoption across sectors.

GS3.headline: Indian Railways' First High-Speed Rail Corridor Achieves 70% Completion
GS3.explainer:
• The Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail (MAHSR) corridor, India's first bullet train project, has achieved 70% physical completion, announced the National High-Speed Rail Corporation Limited (NHSRCL).
• The project involves advanced Japanese Shinkansen technology, aiming for speeds up to 320 kmph, drastically reducing travel time between the two major cities.
• Significant progress has been made on viaducts, bridges, tunnels, and station construction, with meticulous attention to safety and quality standards.
• This ambitious infrastructure project is expected to boost economic development along the corridor, create employment opportunities, and modernize India's railway network.
• The successful implementation of this project is crucial for paving the way for future high-speed rail networks across the country.
